cards.text: Canaan fathered Sidon his firstborn and Heth...
cards.connection: Establishes that the Phoenicians (founders of Carthage) were descendants of Canaan.
cards.cueContext: Used when discussing the origins of Carthage.

cards.text: They have built the high places of Baal to burn their sons in the fire as burnt offerings to Baal, which I did not command or decree...
cards.connection: A biblical condemnation of the very practices (like child sacrifice) found archaeologically at the Tophet in Carthage.
cards.cueContext: Shown during the discussion of Carthage's religion.

cards.text: Come up, O horses, and rage, O chariots! And let the mighty men come forth: the Ethiopians and the Libyans (Put) who handle the shield...
cards.connection: Biblically identifies the descendants of Phut as light-shield warriors, accurately matching the historical Numidian cavalry.
cards.cueContext: Displayed when introducing the Berber kingdoms.

cards.text: who led you through the great and terrifying wilderness... who brought you water out of the flinty rock.
cards.connection: Thematically connects God's provision in the wilderness to the foggara system created by the Garamantes under the dominion mandate.
cards.cueContext: Used when discussing the Foggara miracle of the Garamantes.
